description:
A cloud-native microservice called “inventory-service” is going to be moved from an old
    PostgreSQL instance to a new one as part of an internal cluster migration.
    Your goal is to make a **single, targeted configuration change** and then record a
    **migration log entry**.

    Starting state
    1. The file “/home/user/microservices/inventory-service/config.json” already exists and
       currently contains (among other fields) the line  
       "dbHost": "db.internal.old-cluster.local"
       The rest of the file must remain byte-for-byte identical after your edit; only
       that hostname should change.
    2. The directory “/home/user/migration_logs/” may or may not exist.

    Required actions  
    a. Edit /home/user/microservices/inventory-service/config.json so that the value of
       “dbHost” becomes exactly  
          db.internal.new-cluster.local  
       (everything else in the file must stay untouched, including spacing and new-lines).
    b. Produce or append a **single-line** JSON log record to
       /home/user/migration_logs/inventory-service-move.log that captures the change.

       • The log entry must be a *one-line* JSON object with the exact keys shown below  
         (key order must also match this order so automated tests can parse it):

         {"timestamp":"<ISO8601>","service":"inventory-service","updatedField":"dbHost","oldValue":"db.internal.old-cluster.local","newValue":"db.internal.new-cluster.local"}

         where
           – <ISO8601> is the current UTC timestamp in full
             extended ISO-8601 format, e.g. 2024-03-28T14:07:53Z  
           – No extra whitespace is allowed anywhere in the line.
           – The line must end with a single UNIX newline character (LF).

    Constraints
    • Accomplish everything via normal shell commands; no interactive editors
      such as vim or nano are allowed.  
    • You should only need about two or three commands in total to do this work.

    Verification performed by the grader
    1. It will confirm that /home/user/microservices/inventory-service/config.json now
       contains exactly "dbHost": "db.internal.new-cluster.local".  
    2. It will verify that /home/user/migration_logs/inventory-service-move.log exists,
       is not empty, and that its final line equals the JSON object described above
       (the timestamp value can be any valid ISO-8601 timestamp).  
    3. No other modifications to the filesystem are expected.

    Hint
    • Typical tools you might use include `sed`, `jq`, or `printf >>`.
    • Remember to create the migration_logs directory if it is missing.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:
    Path: /home/user/microservices/inventory-service/config.json
    Contents (exact, including line breaks):

{
  "serviceName": "inventory-service",
  "port": 8080,
  "dbHost": "db.internal.old-cluster.local",
  "dbPort": 5432,
  "logLevel": "INFO"
}

    Expected final state:
    1. /home/user/microservices/inventory-service/config.json differs from the initial
       version by only one substring:
           "dbHost": "db.internal.new-cluster.local"
       Everything else (spacing, line order) remains identical.
    2. /home/user/migration_logs/inventory-service-move.log exists and its very last
       line matches the regular expression (POSIX ERE):

^\{"timestamp":"[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z","service":"inventory-service","updatedField":"dbHost","oldValue":"db\.internal\.old-cluster\.local","newValue":"db\.internal\.new-cluster\.local"\}\n$

       i.e. exactly the one-line JSON object described in the task,
       terminated by a single newline.
name: task_000000_88ce81c9
